$comment: Single source of truth for ALL branding. Edit this, run `pnpm brand:apply`, and every consumer (Capacitor configs, electron-builder, Info.plist, entitlements, web manifest, Swift constants, root package.json, README badges) is regenerated. Adding a new consumer? Add a function to scripts/native/apply-brand.mjs.
name: heron
description: Heron is a thinking partner for career transitions. Local-first job-search platform: pipeline tracking, A–F role evaluation, ATS-optimized CVs, 11-portal scanning, recruiter email triage, interview prep, and opt-in autonomous apply. Runs on macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, iPadOS, Android, and Apple Watch.

identifiers.$comment: capacitorPluginName: bridge contract between TS (registerPlugin) and Swift (jsName). Must match exactly on both sides; apply-brand generates BRAND.capacitorPluginName + Brand.capacitorPluginName so a rebrand retargets both consumers in one step.

extensions.$comment: Extension TARGET names (CareerOpsWidget etc.) are deferred to Task 10 — renaming requires Xcode folder rename + project.pbxproj edits beyond what apply-brand touches. Bundle IDs do flow through (com.heron.app.widget etc.), so the user-facing identity is already Heron post-Task-9.

colors.$comment: Heron 9-key palette. Spec: branding/COLORS.md. The full 22-token CSS system in ui/src/app.css derives from these 9 bases (status colors and text scale are static across brands).

splash.$comment: Heron darkBg — must match capacitor.config.ts → plugins.SplashScreen.backgroundColor so the launch-screen storyboard and the @capacitor/splash-screen overlay look identical.

originalAuthor.name: santifer
originalAuthor.comment: Original creator of the upstream pre-fork project — Heron builds on their work. Credited in docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md and the README upstream link. Do not remove.

permissions.localNetworkUsage: Heron uses local network discovery to find your desktop app on the same Wi-Fi network so your phone, watch, and laptop stay in sync without a cloud account.
permissions.faceIdUsage: Heron uses Face ID to unlock sensitive screens — interview notes, negotiation playbooks, and offer comparisons — so a glance over your shoulder never leaks personal data.
permissions.cameraUsage: Heron uses the camera so you can scan business cards from interviews directly into your contacts file.
permissions.microphoneUsage: Heron uses the microphone for the mock-interview rehearsal mode — audio is processed on-device and never uploaded.
permissions.photoLibraryUsage: Heron needs photo-library access to attach screenshots of job postings or reference letters to a job entry.
permissions.calendarsUsage: Heron reads your calendar to surface upcoming interviews and to schedule follow-up reminders.
permissions.remindersUsage: Heron creates reminders for follow-ups, application deadlines, and interview prep blocks.
permissions.contactsUsage: Heron reads contacts so you can send outreach to recruiters and hiring managers you already know.
permissions.userTrackingUsage: Heron never tracks you. This prompt only appears because Apple requires it for any app that can render web content — we always respond with denied.

copyright: © 2026 Heron contributors (original work © 2026 santifer). Licensed under the MIT License.

appStore.subtitle: A thinking partner for careers
appStore.reviewerNotes: Heron is a personal job-search productivity tool. The app reads job postings the user pastes, drafts tailored CVs/cover letters locally, and tracks application status in a local SQLite database. No personal data leaves the device unless the user explicitly signs in with their own LLM API key (Anthropic / OpenAI / Google). Demo account: not applicable — the app is single-user / self-hosted by design.
appStore.promotionalText: Stand still. Strike well. A patient, local-first thinking partner for career transitions — your data stays on your device.
appStore.marketingText: Tailored CVs, 11-portal scanning, recruiter triage, interview prep. One offline-first dashboard for the moves that matter.

playStore.shortDescription: A thinking partner for career transitions. Local-first, AI-agnostic, private.
playStore.fullDescription: Heron is a patient, local-first thinking partner for career transitions. Tailor CVs and cover letters per role with on-device LLMs, scan 11+ job portals (LinkedIn, Greenhouse, Ashby, Lever, Workday, …), track every application in a local SQLite database, and prep interviews with mock-question + STAR-story drills. Data stays on your device unless you sign in with your own LLM API key. Open-source, MIT-licensed.
